title: Auto Purchase Page Fix Log
fixes_applied_in_content: Added deeper secured vs unsecured loan explanation with default/repossession comparison
Added dealer financing negotiation tips (unique value)
Added GAP insurance FAQ for auto loans
Strengthened demand evidence with autocomplete verification

new_content_added.secured_vs_unsecured_deep_dive: If you default on an unsecured $30K personal loan, the lender can sue you and garnish wages. If you default on a secured auto loan, they repossess the car — but you still owe the deficiency balance if the car sells for less than the loan. This is why secured loans have lower rates.
new_content_added.dealer_financing_tips: Dealerships profit from financing. They may offer you 9% when you qualify for 7% elsewhere. Always get pre-approved from a credit union or bank BEFORE visiting the dealer. Use the pre-approval as leverage — tell the dealer: 'I'm pre-approved at 7%. Can you beat that?'
new_content_added.gap_insurance_faq: If you finance a $30K car and it's totaled in year 1, your insurance pays market value (~$24K) but you still owe ~$28K on the loan. GAP insurance covers the $4K difference. It costs $500-700 — worth it for loans over 80% of the car's value.
new_content_added.apr_sensitivity_table: Table showing $30K at 5%, 6%, 7%, 8%, 9%, 10% — monthly payment and total cost differences

deployment: NOT in this batch — held for Phase 32.3 or later
note: Content fixes applied to the page brief. Will be built as a Next.js route in the next batch.
